item.title: ભારતીય રિયલ એસ્ટેટમાં 10 કરોડ નવા ઘરોની માંગ: રોકાણનો યોગ્ય સમય અને સાચી પસંદગી

item.title: How the Bhatta-Parsaul Protests Reshaped Land Laws and Real Estate in India
item.description: The Bhatta-Parsaul protests of 2011 exposed the deep flaws in India’s colonial-era land acquisition system. The violent clashes in Greater Noida highlighted how farmers were receiving poor compensation and limited rehabilitation, forcing a nationwide rethink on land policies. This unrest directly influenced the introduction of the Right to Fair Compensation and Transparency in Land Acquisition, Rehabilitation and Resettlement Act (LARR) of 2013.

item.title: How the 2025 ITC Removal is Reshaping Commercial Real Estate Yields
item.description: The removal of Input Tax Credit (ITC) for leasing commercial properties in 2025 has introduced a significant shift in India’s real estate landscape. This policy change has compressed net rental yields by approximately 0.5–1% on new projects. For investors and tenants navigating this evolving market, understanding these dynamics is essential for making informed, profitable decisions.

item.title: How Interest Rates Influence Commercial Real Estate Valuations in India
item.description: Interest rate fluctuations are a primary driver of commercial real estate (CRE) values, directly affecting borrowing costs, investor demand, and the potential income from a property. Generally, rising interest rates increase the cost of financing, dampen buyer appetite, and exert downward pressure on property values due to higher cap rates. Conversely, falling rates make borrowing more affordable, attract investors, compress cap rates, and drive property values up. In essence, interest rates act as a critical lever that dictates CRE valuations, development activity, and long-term market sentiment.

item.title: Demystifying the Loan-to-Value (LTV) Ratio in Indian Real Estate
item.description: The Loan-to-Value (LTV) ratio is a critical metric that measures the proportion of a property’s value that is financed through a loan. Simply put, it is calculated as (Loan Amount ÷ Appraised Property Value) × 100. In the Indian real estate market, understanding your LTV is essential for securing home loans, negotiating interest rates, and assessing financial risk. A lower LTV (generally below 80%) often signals stronger financial health, leading to easier loan approvals and better terms, while a higher LTV can increase costs and risk.

item.title: Converting Agricultural Land to Residential Land in Gujarat: A Step-by-Step Guide
item.description: To convert agricultural land into residential land in Gujarat, you must obtain NA (Non-Agricultural) permission under Section 65 of the Gujarat Land Revenue Code, 1879. The process involves applying to the District Collector, submitting land documents, paying conversion fees, and securing layout and zoning approvals from the local Urban Development Authority or Municipality. Once approved, the land officially becomes Non-Agricultural (Residential) and can be used for housing or real estate projects.

item.title: How to Buy Agricultural Land in Gujarat: Rules, Process, and Smart Investment Tips
item.description: To purchase agricultural land in Gujarat, you must typically hold an agricultural status. Non-farmers, including urban investors and NRIs, face restrictions under the Gujarat Tenancy and Agricultural Lands Act, 1948. However, pathways exist through inheritance, obtaining NA (Non-Agricultural) permission, or securing agriculturist certification. As with any property transaction, verifying the title, understanding zone classifications, and ensuring legal compliance are paramount to a secure investment.

item.title: DLF’s ₹16,000 Crore Milestone: Analyzing the Surge in Gurgaon’s Ultra-Luxury Market
item.description: DLF Ltd has recently set a remarkable benchmark in the Indian real estate sector by selling 221 ultra-luxury apartments at The Dahlias, located in DLF Phase 5, Gurugram. The bookings have reached approximately ₹15,818 crore (around ₹72 crore per unit) by the September quarter of FY26. This record-breaking sale underscores the robust demand for super-premium housing in the NCR region and highlights the dominance of credible developers in the luxury segment.

item.title: Gurugram Metro Expansion: Route, Timeline, and Real Estate Impact
item.description: The new Gurugram Metro corridor connecting HUDA City Centre to Cyber City is more than just a transport upgrade; it represents a fundamental shift in the city’s connectivity and real estate landscape. item.title: Mutation of Agricultural Land: A Guide to Transparent Investment in Rural India
item.description: The mutation of agricultural land—the process of updating official records to reflect a change in ownership or land use—is becoming a critical factor for real estate investments in rural India. With the rise of digital record-keeping, expanding infrastructure corridors, and the conversion of agricultural plots to non-agricultural (NA) status, new opportunities are emerging for discerning investors. At 360 Ghar, we believe that just as technology brings transparency to property discovery, understanding the legalities of mutation brings clarity to land investment. Whether you are a homebuyer, an NRI, or an investor, keeping an eye on mutation status and land-use conversion rules is essential to identifying high-potential parcels with confidence.

item.description: When you finally secure a property—whether through purchase, inheritance, or gift—the journey isn't quite over. By enabling fractional ownership of income-generating Grade A and Grade B commercial properties, these instruments allow investors to participate in high-quality office, retail, and logistics assets without the need to purchase an entire building.

item.title: How Rising Gold and Silver Prices Impact Your Home Buying Power
item.description: High precious metal prices affect homebuyer affordability by tightening household budgets, influencing inflation expectations, and pushing interest rates higher. When gold and silver prices rise, they often signal economic uncertainty, which can lead to cautious lending and higher borrowing costs. This combination reduces purchasing power, increases monthly home loan payments, and delays buying decisions. For many buyers, especially first-time buyers, elevated precious metal prices indirectly make homes less affordable even when property prices stay stable.

item.title: Silver Investment vs. Real Estate: Returns, Risk, and Reality
item.description: Investing in silver currently offers higher short-term liquidity and explosive growth potential compared to the stable, albeit slower-moving, real estate market. While silver has surged by over 270% in the past year—reaching record highs of Rs 3.75 lakh per kg—real estate remains a robust long-term hedge, typically delivering average annual returns of 5% to 7%. For investors seeking rapid capital appreciation in a volatile 2026 economy, silver is a compelling choice. Conversely, real estate remains the superior option for those prioritizing rental income, physical security, and tangible asset ownership.

item.title: How Gold and Silver Price Movements Influence Real Estate Demand and Property Prices
item.description: Gold and silver prices influence real estate demand by shifting investor behavior, liquidity, and risk appetite. When precious metal prices rise, investors often rebalance profits into property as a stable, income-generating asset. Conversely, when gold and silver fall or move sideways, real estate demand can slow as capital remains parked in metals. These movements affect property prices, buyer sentiment, and long-term investment cycles across residential and commercial markets.

item.title: The Strategic Advantage of Dwarka Expressway: How IGI Airport Connectivity is Driving Real Estate Value
item.description: Living in Sector 113 on Dwarka Expressway has fundamentally redefined what airport proximity means in the NCR real estate market. What was once marketed as future potential is now a verified daily reality. Residents can leave home and reach IGI Airport Terminal 3 in approximately 20 minutes without encountering a single traffic signal. This level of efficiency has shifted buyer preference away from congested legacy corridors toward infrastructure-led micro-markets. While Golf Course Extension Road remains a premium address, its airport commute often exceeds an hour during peak traffic. Dwarka Expressway, supported by the Urban Extension Road II and emerging commercial hubs, has positioned Sector 113 as a new gateway between Gurgaon, Delhi, and the world beyond.

This progress underscores a robust momentum toward the national target of delivering 4.95 crore pucca houses equipped with basic amenities by March 2029. By combining financial assistance with multi-scheme convergence, the program is transforming the rural landscape, offering improved social security and standardized living conditions for millions of households across the country.

item.title: Essential RERA Provisions Every Homebuyer in India Must Know
item.description: The Real Estate (Regulation and Development) Act, 2016 (RERA) was enacted to bring transparency, accountability, and fairness to India’s real estate sector. Its provisions safeguard homebuyers by mandating project registration, strict fund management, standardized agreements, timely delivery, and effective grievance redressal.

item.title: Tax Benefits on Home Loans in India: A Complete Guide
item.description: Home loan tax benefits in India offer substantial relief to borrowers under the Income Tax Act, 1961. By claiming deductions of up to ₹2 lakh on home loan interest under Section 24(b) and up to ₹1.5 lakh on principal repayment under Section 80C, you can significantly reduce your tax liability. Additional provisions, such as Sections 80EE and 80EEA, offer further support to first-time buyers.

item.title: Impact of Revised Circle Rates on Delhi Property: What Buyers Must Know
item.description: Revised circle rates in Delhi play a pivotal role in determining the actual cost of acquiring property. As a platform committed to transparency and convenience, 360 Ghar believes that understanding these government-notified rates is essential for making informed real estate decisions. Circle rates act as the minimum valuation benchmark for calculating stamp duty and registration charges. When these rates are revised upwards, they directly increase the transaction costs for buyers, often inflating the overall tax burden and prompting sellers to adjust their asking prices to match the new benchmarks. This dynamic can make housing less affordable for middle and lower-income groups and potentially slow down transaction volumes in high-demand areas. Conversely, a reduction in circle rates—such as the 20% cut implemented in the past—can significantly improve affordability, spur market activity, and offer relief to buyers relying on home loans.

item.title: Indian Real Estate Market Stabilizes: Housing Price Growth Slows to 6%
item.description: The Indian housing market entered a phase of healthy normalization in 2025, with average residential price growth cooling to 6%. This marks a significant deceleration from the 17% surge witnessed in 2024. However, this broader cooling is not uniform across the nation. Southern tech hubs like Bengaluru and Hyderabad have defied the trend with robust growth of 13% and 8%, respectively, driven by steady end-user demand. At 360 Ghar, we view this transition as a shift toward a more disciplined market where developers are prioritizing price integrity over high-volume liquidation. For homebuyers, this creates an environment where transparency and verified information become essential tools for making sound investment decisions.

item.title: MahaRERA’s Landmark Order: Empowering Homebuyers to Take Over Stalled Projects
item.description: In a significant move for property rights and consumer protection, the Maharashtra Real Estate Regulatory Authority (MahaRERA) has passed a landmark order allowing homebuyers to take over the development rights of a severely delayed housing project. By revoking the builder’s registration for the Clan City project near Taloja, the authority has effectively shifted control from a non-compliant promoter to the association of allottees. This decision strengthens the protective framework of RERA and sets a powerful precedent for how stalled real estate projects can be resolved across Maharashtra. At 360 Ghar, we believe that transparency and accountability are the cornerstones of a healthy real estate ecosystem, and this ruling reinforces the importance of regulatory oversight in safeguarding homebuyer interests.

item.description: The real estate sector thrives on clarity, transparency, and the assurance that due process will be followed. item.title: Fractional Ownership in Commercial Property: A 2025 Guide for Smart Investors
item.description: Fractional ownership in commercial property allows multiple investors to jointly own a high-value real estate asset, such as an office tower, retail mall, or warehouse. Instead of needing crores to buy a single property, investors can enter the market with smaller amounts—starting from ₹10–25 lakh—while enjoying rental income, capital appreciation, and shared ownership rights.

item.title: The Bhatta-Parsaul Turning Point: How It Shaped Yamuna Expressway’s Real Estate Landscape
item.description: The Bhatta-Parsaul incident of May 2011 was a watershed moment in Indian real estate history. What began as a farmer protest in the twin villages of Bhatta and Parsaul near Greater Noida soon escalated into a national debate on land rights. The conflict arose over land acquisition for the Yamuna Expressway, with farmers demanding fair compensation and rehabilitation. The resulting clashes and political outcry forced the government to re-evaluate the colonial-era Land Acquisition Act of 1894, eventually leading to the more farmer-friendly Land Acquisition, Rehabilitation, and Resettlement (LARR) Act of 2013.

item.title: How the Delhi-Meerut RRTS is Reshaping Real Estate Opportunities in the NCR
item.description: The full operationalization of the Delhi–Meerut Regional Rapid Transit System (RRTS) marks a transformative moment for regional connectivity in the National Capital Region (NCR). Inaugurated on February 22, 2026, this high-speed corridor has reduced travel time between Sarai Kale Khan and Meerut to under 60 minutes. Covering 82 kilometers, the Namo Bharat network is not just a transit upgrade; it is a robust economic engine driving real estate demand. Micro-markets in Meerut and Ghaziabad are witnessing significant appreciation, with property values rising by 30% to 131% over the past four years. As transit-oriented development takes hold, peripheral towns are rapidly evolving into viable residential hubs for Delhi's workforce.

Spanning approximately 6,000 square feet, this 4BHK apartment is valued at around ₹30 crore. It is particularly renowned for its expansive 270-degree views of the Arabian Sea. Designed by the global firm Wilson Associates, the home integrates high-end automation, a private library, and a dedicated fitness zone. item.title: Floor Area Ratio (FAR) & Floor Space Index (FSI): A Complete Guide
Understanding Super Built-up Area: Meaning, Formula, and "Loading"
item.description:
Carpet Area – Actual usable floor space measured wall-to-wall; helps you understand interior usability and day-to-day comfort.
Built-up Area – Carpet area plus walls and balconies; mainly used for government approvals and tax calculations.
Super Built-up Area – Built-up area along with a proportionate share of common amenities; commonly used for pricing and marketing purposes.
By understanding the Super Built-up Area and the loading factor, you can make informed decisions and ensure you’re paying for genuine usable space and shared facilities—not just an inflated number in a brochure.

item.title: Understanding Built-up Area: Meaning, Formula, and Calculation
item.description:
Understanding the difference between carpet area vs built-up area is crucial when buying or evaluating a property. The carpet area represents the actual usable space inside a home, including internal rooms and walls, while excluding balconies, external walls, and common areas. On the other hand, the built-up area includes the carpet area along with balconies and external walls, offering a broader measurement of the property size.
Knowing these differences helps homebuyers compare property prices accurately, make informed real-estate decisions, and ensure compliance with property tax and legal regulations. This knowledge is essential for avoiding hidden costs and understanding the true value of a residential property.

item.title: Understanding Carpet Area Under RERA: A Comprehensive Guide for Property Buyers
item.description: As per RERA, the carpet area includes only the actual usable space inside the home, such as bedrooms, living room, kitchen, and internal walls. It excludes balconies, terraces, external walls, and common areas. Focusing on carpet area ensures you pay only for the space you truly live in, not inflated “super area” figures.
